name: Donovan Edwards
headline: Deep Dolphins depth piece with no path to touches yet
body:
Edwards joined Miami off Washington's practice squad and sits at the very bottom of a crowded backfield, buried behind De'Von Achane with Jaylen Wright and Ollie Gordon also ahead of him. The consensus pegs him as roughly the 140th running back, ECR draft rank #502, and rostered in essentially zero leagues. His arrival was a roster-shuffle move tied to Darren Waller landing on IR, not a signal of any expanded role, and the projections reflect it: about 31 carries for 130 yards and a score plus two catches, totaling under 21 fantasy points for the year.

There is no realistic standalone value here right now. The 23-year-old former Michigan back is a watch-list name only, someone whose situation could shift if an injury opens snaps in front of him. Until that happens, leave him on waivers and revisit only if the depth chart thins out.
